executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware assault with over 100 organizations compromised in a single 48-hour period. High-profile victims include Canada Goose, National Money Mart (database leaked), Peterborough Public Health, Eastern Townships School Board, Municipality of North Perth, and Workers Health & Safety Centre. At least 20 distinct ransomware groups are actively targeting Canadian entities across all sectors, with Qilin (23 victims) and CLOP (13 victims) leading the campaign. Critical infrastructure including public health, education, telecommunications, and financial services have been breached.

items.name: Qilin
items.targets: 23 Canadian organizations including Canada Goose subsidiary (Kuzco Lighting), Eastern Townships School Board, Workers Health & Safety Centre, NovAtel (Hexagon), BioPharma Services, Waterloo Information Systems, and multiple SMEs across construction, manufacturing, technology, and financial services
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service) via leak sites, T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery)

items.name: CLOP
items.targets: 13 Canadian organizations including law firms (smtlawyers.ca), technology providers (clouddataworks.ca, sensiblesolutions.ca, technikel.ca, modtech.ca), energy sector (eastplats.com), hospitality (granitepointe.ca, whiskijackresorts.com), and manufacturing (idealwelders.com)
items.ttps: T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), T1048 (Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol), T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), historically associated with mass exploitation campaigns

items.name: Play
items.targets: 11 Canadian organizations including Equine Canada, Chemical Computing Group, Makivik, Security ONE Alarm Systems, multiple manufacturing firms (Paisley Products, Select Tool, Katch Kan, Turkstra Trusses), and financial services (Pewarchuk CPA)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter), T1078 (Valid Accounts)

items.name: SafePay
items.targets: 11 Canadian organizations primarily in construction (groupepiche.ca, silverlinegroupinc.com, kenalex.ca), agriculture/food (mattandsteve.com, ripobec.com), transportation (briwaycarriers.com), education (teccart.qc.ca), and manufacturing (precisionaluminum.ca)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service)

items.name: DragonForce
items.targets: 6 Canadian organizations including technology providers (phoenixlabs.com, CCR Solutions), construction (Esposito Bros. Construction), education publishing (Emond Publishing), agriculture (soteck.com), and healthcare (McKee-Pownall Equine Services)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service)

items.name: INCRansom
items.targets: 8 Canadian organizations in construction (excavationtourigny.ca), manufacturing (CPK Interior, steelworksinc.ca, omegatoolcorp.com), consumer services (glasserstv.com), healthcare (bisonfamilymedical.com), financial services (reidhurstnagy.com), and education (baisyaakov.ca)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1005 (Data from Local System), T1041 (Exfiltration Over C2 Channel)

items.name: TheGentlemen
items.targets: 5 Canadian organizations including healthcare (Labtician Ophthalmics), construction (Groupe Caddac), manufacturing (ControlGMC, Real Tech), and business services (Colliers)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service)

items.name: Lynx
items.targets: 5 Canadian organizations including Peterborough Public Health, technology (ktlgroup.com), manufacturing (laurysenkitchens.com), business services (groupecho.com), and financial services (Trucash)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), targeting public sector and critical services

items.name: Sinobi
items.targets: 3 Canadian organizations including Active Green + Ross (automotive retail), Morison Insurance Brokers, and Kids & Company (childcare services)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service)

items.name: AiLock
items.targets: 2 Canadian organizations: Solutions Extreme Technology and Eeyou Communications Network (telecommunications serving Indigenous communities)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1489 (Service Stop)

items.name: Devman
items.targets: 4 Canadian organizations including Axion50plus (financial services), beausejourco-op.crs (agriculture), and ravand.com (technology)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1005 (Data from Local System)

items.name: Everest
items.targets: National Money Mart Company with confirmed database leak (financial services - payday loans/cheque cashing)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1530 (Data from Cloud Storage), confirmed data exfiltration and publication

items.name: Akira
items.targets: Inland Audio Visual (business services)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1078 (Valid Accounts)

items.name: Nightspire
items.targets: 2 Canadian organizations: Regal Building Materials Ltd. (construction) and one unidentified organization
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service)

items.name: Bravox
items.targets: John O's Foods (agriculture/food production)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1005 (Data from Local System)

items.name: CoinbaseCartel
items.targets: Canada Goose (major Canadian consumer brand and manufacturer)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service)

items.name: Payload
items.targets: Q2 Artificial Lift Services (manufacturing)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery)

items.name: Nitrogen
items.targets: Whitfield Welding Inc (manufacturing)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1005 (Data from Local System)

items.name: Securotrop
items.targets: 2 Canadian organizations: Living Realty (business services) and Cadman Power Equipment (manufacturing)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service)

items.name: Medusa
items.targets: MRC de Maskinongé district (public sector municipal government in Quebec)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1078 (Valid Accounts), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service)

items.name: WorldLeaks
items.targets: Municipality of North Perth (public sector local government)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service)

items.name: Anubis
items.targets: One unidentified Canadian law firm
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1005 (Data from Local System)

items.name: MedusaLocker
items.targets: dulay.ca (technology)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery)

canada_landscape.summary: Canada is facing a massive coordinated ransomware campaign with 100+ organizations breached across all provinces and sectors on June 24, 2026. Critical infrastructure compromised includes public health (Peterborough Public Health), education (Eastern Townships School Board, Teccart College, Bais Yaakov), municipal government (North Perth, MRC de Maskinongé), telecommunications (Eeyou Communications Network), and worker safety organizations. No CCCS advisories were identified in the reporting period.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose (via subsidiary Kuzco Lighting) and Active Green + Ross automotive retail compromised. Kids & Company childcare services also breached.
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company (payday loans/financial services) suffered confirmed database leak via Everest ransomware. Morison Insurance Brokers, Trucash, and multiple accounting firms (Pewarchuk CPA, MG Chartered Professional Accountant, reidhurstnagy.com) also compromised. No major banks (RBC, TD, Scotiabank, BMO, CIBC) identified.

report_2w.executive_summary: CRITICAL: The CCCS issued Alert AL26-014 for 'FortiBleed,' a massive credential leak exposing over 86,000 compromised Fortinet device credentials—urgent hardening required for all internet-facing Fortinet assets. Multiple zero-day vulnerabilities are being actively exploited: Palo Alto Networks GlobalProtect VPN authentication bypass, Check Point VPN exploited by Qilin ransomware, and an Exchange OWA XSS zero-day. The threat group 'Icarus' claimed responsibility for a growing OAuth breach affecting Klue and its customers including Salesforce users. A 24 billion credential database (8TB) was discovered exposed in an unprotected Elasticsearch instance, compiled from infostealer malware campaigns.

items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: Apache HTTP Server path traversal and RCE vulnerability
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: prefecthq/prefect 3.6.23 allows users with deployment creation rights to inject git flags via commit_sha/directory parameters
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High
items.access_required: Authenticated user with deployment creation rights

items.name: Icarus
items.targets: Klue, Salesforce customers
items.ttps: OAuth credential theft, T1078 (Valid Accounts), T1566 (Phishing), data extortion

items.name: Qilin Ransomware
items.targets: Organizations using Check Point VPN
items.ttps: T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), zero-day exploitation of Check Point VPN

items.name: Handala Group
items.targets: California Water Service systems
items.ttps: T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), T1530 (Data from Cloud Storage Object)

items.name: SocGholish
items.targets: WordPress blogs, compromised websites
items.ttps: T1189 (Drive-by Compromise), T1204 (User Execution), malware distribution via fake browser updates

items.name: GentleKiller (Gentlemen RaaS)
items.targets: Various organizations
items.ttps: T1068 (Exploitation for Privilege Escalation), T1562.001 (Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools), BYOVD (Bring Your Own Vulnerable Driver), EDR evasion

items.name: NFCShare Android Malware
items.targets: Android users via fake banking app updates
items.ttps: T1476 (Deliver Malicious App via Other Means), T1444 (Masquerade as Legitimate Application), distribution via GitHub

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued Alert AL26-014 warning of the 'FortiBleed' credential leak affecting thousands of Fortinet devices, with urgent guidance to harden internet-facing assets. Canada passed Bill C-22, expanding lawful access powers for law enforcement to obtain digital information under judicial warrants, raising privacy concerns among advocacy groups. No direct incidents affecting major Canadian retailers or financial institutions were identified in this reporting period.
